evals.name: full-pipeline-inline-prompt
evals.prompt: Add a configurable rate limiter to the /api/upload and /api/download endpoints with per-user limits stored in Redis.
evals.expected_output: All 8 pipeline stages executed in order, spec and plan created in docs/spec/, implementation with tests, quality gate passed, PR created
expectations: Announces 'Using the orchestrate skill to run the full development pipeline' at the start
DAG dashboard is initialized before any Skill or Agent call, with spec name derived from the prompt
Stage 0 (Setup) runs in main conversation using the pipeline-setup skill via Skill tool and stores WORKTREE_PATH, BRANCH_NAME, SPEC_NAME
Stage 1 (Brainstorm) runs in main conversation using the brainstorm and spec-generation skills via Skill tool, producing docs/spec/<name>/spec.md
Stage 2 (Planner) runs in main conversation using the planning skill via Skill tool, producing docs/spec/<name>/plan.md and phase-*.md files
Stage 3 (Coder) dispatches phases as sub-agents via Agent tool, not in the main conversation
Every sub-agent prompt includes the preamble with WORKTREE_PATH
dag-update set-status is called for each stage transition (running/done/failed/skipped)
anti_expectations: Does not dispatch Stages 0, 1, or 2 as sub-agents via Agent tool
Does not proceed to Stage 5 (Sync Docs) if Quality Gate returns BLOCKED or STAGNATION
Does not skip the Quality Gate stage under any circumstances
Does not skip Stage 7 (Commit & PR) under any circumstances

evals.name: spec-file-input-with-skippable-stages
evals.prompt: Run the full pipeline using the spec at docs/spec/user-auth/spec.md — it already has a complete problem definition, acceptance criteria, and file-level change instructions. Skip brainstorm.
evals.expected_output: Brainstorm stage skipped with reason logged, planner evaluates whether to skip based on spec completeness, mandatory stages all execute, DAG nodes for skipped stages set to skipped status
expectations: Detects the argument as a file path by checking if the file exists using Bash
Reads the spec file contents and stores as TASK_CONTEXT
Skips Stage 1 (Brainstorm) because the caller explicitly says 'skip brainstorm', and sets DAG status to skipped
Logs a message explaining why Stage 1 was skipped with the reason
Stage 0 (Setup) still executes even though brainstorm is skipped — mandatory stages are never skipped
Stage 4 (Quality Gate) executes unconditionally as a mandatory stage
Stage 3 (Coder) executes unconditionally as a mandatory stage
A compact pipeline summary table is presented after all stages complete
anti_expectations: Does not skip Stage 0 (Setup) even if other stages are skipped
Does not skip Stage 3 (Coder), Stage 4 (Quality Gate), Stage 6 (Learnings), or Stage 7 (Commit & PR)
Does not proceed past a BLOCKED quality gate verdict to Stage 5 or beyond

evals.name: parallel-phase-dispatch-and-error-halt
evals.prompt: Refactor the payment processing module to support multiple payment providers (Stripe, PayPal, Square) with a common interface and provider-specific adapters.
evals.expected_output: Multi-phase plan generated, independent phases dispatched in parallel via multiple Agent calls in a single message, pipeline halts if any sub-agent fails
expectations: After Stage 2 (Planner), reads the phase graph (DOT digraph) from plan.md to determine phase dependencies
Dispatches ready phases (no incomplete predecessors) in parallel by sending multiple Agent tool calls in a single message
Phase nodes are added as children of the coder node in the DAG dashboard after planning completes
Each phase sub-agent prompt includes the spec path, plan path, and phase-specific instructions
Each coder sub-agent is instructed to invoke the tdd skill
After all phases complete, dag-update set-status coder is set to done
Pipeline summary includes phase count, parallel count, files changed, tests passing, and PR URL
Dashboard is finalized with dag-update finalize done after the summary
anti_expectations: Does not dispatch dependent phases before their predecessors complete
Does not continue the pipeline if any sub-agent fails or returns an error
Does not retry if quality gate returns STAGNATION — stops entirely and reports manual intervention is needed
